findings: No abnormal signal intensity within the brain parenchyma. The ventricles and sulci are normal in size and configuration. No midline shift. No extra-axial fluid collection. The major intracranial flow voids are preserved.

Small T2 hyperintense lesion in the left frontal lobe white matter measuring approximately 5 mm. No surrounding edema. No restricted diffusion on DWI. Remainder of the brain parenchyma is unremarkable.

Multiple scattered T2/FLAIR hyperintense foci in the periventricular and subcortical white matter bilaterally, some with ovoid morphology and perpendicular orientation to the ventricles. A few lesions demonstrate incomplete ring enhancement. No restricted diffusion. Mild prominence of the lateral ventricles.

Post-surgical changes in the right temporal lobe with resection cavity measuring approximately 3 x 2 cm. Residual enhancing nodular tissue along the medial margin of the cavity. Surrounding vasogenic edema extending into the right insular cortex and right basal ganglia. Mild mass effect with effacement of the right temporal horn. No midline shift. Left hemisphere unremarkable.

Acute infarct in the left MCA territory involving the left insular cortex, left temporal lobe, and left frontal operculum with restricted diffusion on DWI/ADC maps. Associated cortical swelling with effacement of the adjacent sulci. No hemorrhagic transformation. Left M1 segment shows reduced flow void suggesting occlusion. Remainder of the intracranial vasculature appears patent.
